claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch has a broken import path that will cause runtime failure; provider isolation logic exists but the module cannot load

evidence.summary: Line 4 imports normalizeAgentProvider from ../adapters/agent-contract.js which does not exist; src/adapters/ directory is absent
evidence.why: This import error prevents evidence-dispatch.ts from loading at runtime

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider function exists in src/runtime/adapters/agent-contract.ts, not in src/adapters/
evidence.why: This shows the correct location of the imported function

evidence.summary: executionConfig function implements provider-specific normalization for claude, codex, and custom providers
evidence.why: This is the provider isolation logic that would work if the import were fixed

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider-specific CLI arguments for codex (lines 43-60), custom (63-75), and claude (77-108)
evidence.why: This shows provider-specific command construction exists

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses git ls-files for efficient tracking and walkFiles as fallback for non-git repos
evidence.why: This is the boundary enforcement core implementation

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: This is the boundary violation detection logic

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot before agent execution and after, then diffs to detect violations
evidence.why: This is the actual boundary enforcement in dispatch runtime

evidence.summary: Test executes explorer, cross-checker, and verifier agents then feeds results into readiness check
evidence.why: This test shows the full evidence agent workflow

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: This test proves boundary enforcement blocks violations

evidence.summary: Doctrine specifies Claude dispatch does not use tool restriction flags; safety comes from prompt contract and boundary snapshots
evidence.why: This explains the security model design intent

risks: The broken import on line 4 of evidence-dispatch.ts will cause any attempt to run demand evidence dispatch to fail at module load time
Previous evidence results from dispatch-20260601033747536-4h57gt may have been generated before this import was broken or with a different file structure
Fixing the import path may reveal additional runtime issues that were masked by the import failure
missing: No evidence that demand evidence dispatch has been successfully run with the current broken import state
No evidence of integration tests running full dispatch against real provider binaries after the import break
No evidence of runtime validation that provider isolation actually prevents cross-provider leakage in live execution

result.notes: A critical import error exists on line 4 of src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts. The file imports from ../adapters/agent-contract.js but the correct path is ../runtime/adapters/agent-contract.ts. This will cause immediate runtime failure when attempting to run demand evidence dispatch. The provider isolation logic appears well-designed in theory, but cannot execute due to this module loading error. Evidence from previous dispatch runs may not reflect the current broken state.
